channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: El costo-beneficio detrás de la continuidad operativa, la estrategia de Spin
item.description: Los respaldos, redundancia en la nube y los planes de continuidad ya no se justifican solo como inversiones técnicas: hoy necesitan demostrar su valor frente al negocio con una evaluación de costo–beneficio donde los indicadores de seguridad demuestren su impacto en la operación. La tendencia reciente es que, más que preguntar cuánto se gastó en seguridad, los directorios y áreas de negocio exigen demostrar qué riesgos se reducen y qué servicios siguen operando. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: El beneficio de las aseguradoras sube un 5% en los tres primeros meses de 2026
item.description: Las estadísticas de Icea muestran un ligero aumento de la siniestralidad media del conjunto de aseguradoras desde el 74,35% de las primas imputadas de negocio directo en marzo de 2025 al 75,34% al cierre de los tres primeros meses de este año. Con un resultado de la cuenta no técnica del 8,04% de las primas imputadas de negocio retenido en el primer trimestre del año (8,34% en el mismo periodo de 2025), las aseguradoras alcanzaron en el inicio del ejercicio un beneficio neto conjunto de 1.696 millones de euros, un 4,7% más que doce meses antes. En líneas generales, las aseguradoras inician el año con una ralentización en el incremento de sus resultados respecto a 2025, un ejercicio que se cerró con una mejora del 15% en las ganancias.

item.title: ¿Son caros los seguros en España? Un tercio de los consumidores cree que sí
item.description: La presión del coste de vida impacta con mayor intensidad en los consumidores españoles que en el resto de mercados europeos analizados en el ‘Estudio Europeo de Consumidores de Seguros 2026′, de Guidewire. En España y Francia, el 26% y el 32%, respectivamente, consideran que los productos del sector son caros. En otra de las cuestiones analizadas, España es el país con mayor proporción de consumidores favorables a que las aseguradoras recopilen información a través de sensores y dispositivos conectados : un 40% valora esta práctica de forma positiva, frente al 36% que la rechaza.

item.title: «El talento senior es absolutamente estratégico»
item.description: Sin embargo, en muchas ocasiones ese talento senior no se aprovecha plenamente o queda relegado a funciones menos estratégicas. Conversamos con ella sobre el valor del talento senior y sobre cómo la diversidad, entendida en toda su amplitud —incluyendo la experiencia generacional—, se ha convertido en un factor clave para la sostenibilidad y competitividad de las organizaciones. Para Olga García Pozo, profesional de Recursos Humanos con una larga trayectoria en el sector bancario y actual voluntaria senior en SECOT, estaba claro que todavía tenía mucho que aportar.

item.title: Insurance AI Is Booming. Fraud Losses Are Not Falling.
item.description: The Insurance Information Institute, citing the Coalition Against Insurance Fraud’s 2022 SIU Benchmarking Study, found that SIU staffing grew just 1.4 percent from 2021 to 2022. Insurers have spent the last three years teaching AI to catch fraud. Most conversations about fraud detection miss this: the documents are often real.

item.title: Tower elevates Michael Skeens to chief operating officer
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden hires FM veteran Manikandan Krishnamoorthy as regional director for risk and engineering advisory
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending May 24th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending May 24th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending May 24th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Beyond the canvas: APAC fine art and specie insurance boom fuelled by rising risks, shifting wealth patterns
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: South Korea’s DB Insurance set to complete Fortegra acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Nationwide expands retirement product leadership team
item.description: Nationwide Retirement Solutions announced that Adam Lacks has been appointed associate vice president (AVP) of Product Development for government markets, effective April 27. In this role, he will report to Hutch Schafer and lead product strategy, design and development for the Retirement Solutions business supported on the DCdirect recordkeeping platform. Lacks will oversee the development of new products, features and capabilities designed to keep Nationwide’s retirement plans business aligned with and ahead of the evolving public sector defined contribution marketplace.

item.title: Energetic Capital expands credit insurance capabilities and capacity
item.description: Energetic Capital announced renewed credit insurance agreements with SCOR and additional capacity from Lloyd's markets led by OAK Global, expanding its underwriting capabilities for clean energy and infrastructure projects. “We see a compelling opportunity to deploy (re)insurance capacity through Energetic Capital’s underwriting platform. With SCOR’s continued support and new capacity from Lloyd’s led by OAK Global, we are well positioned to scale our underwriting and bring flexible solutions to a broader set of infrastructure opportunities.” – Nathan Maggiotto, CEO of Energetic Capital.

item.title: Inter Miami CF partners with Marsh
item.description: Inter Miami CF announced a partnership with Marsh, naming the insurance broker the club’s commercial insurance broker and official partner. Under the agreement, Marsh will provide insurance brokerage services for Inter Miami CF’s operations, including Nu Stadium and the broader Miami Freedom Park development. Marsh will also use Inter Miami CF hospitality assets and matchday experiences for client engagement and business development activities in South Florida.

item.title: Inszone acquires Taylor Insurance Agency in Texas
item.description: Inszone Insurance announced the acquisition of Kevin Taylor Insurance Agency, LLC, operating as Taylor Insurance Agency, expanding its personal lines presence in San Antonio, Texas. “I grew this agency on my own, but I knew I needed Inszone to get to the next level.” – Kevin Taylor, owner of Taylor Insurance Agency. Founded in 2011 as a captive agency before transitioning to an independent model in 2014, Taylor Insurance Agency built a large personal lines portfolio focused on individually generated business.

item.title: Japan’s big 3 make waves globally
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Efectibank recibe licencia en Perú y Klar compra Yave
item.description: Financiera Efectiva migra a Efectibank, nuevo banco digital La empresa financiera peruana recibió la licencia de funcionamiento para operar como banco digital en el país andino, su nueva apuesta por ampliar sus facultades y competir en el segmento. De esta forma, Efectibank se convierte en el primer banco digital peruano con presencia física. Cajas municipales preparan billetera para competir en pagos digitales de Perú Las cajas municipales en Perú avanzan en el desarrollo de una billetera interoperable llamada Toque , con la que buscan ganar participación en el negocio de pagos digitales frente a bancos y fintechs.

item.title: Manulife Announces Executive Leadership Changes
item.description: Source: Manulife Hong Kong.) Manulife (Toronto) has announced changes to its executive and senior leadership teams across Canada, Hong Kong, AI and data, and technology and operations as the company executes its refreshed enterprise strategy. “These important leadership changes ensure we have the right capabilities, both at the enterprise level and in our key markets of Canada and Hong Kong, to achieve our bold ambition and deliver against our new strategic priorities,” comments Phil Witherington, President and CEO, Manulife. I am confident that we have the right leadership team to deliver on Manulife’s next chapter of long-term growth.” The changes are effective July 1, 2026.

item.title: Banorte Extends Risk Management with AI Signals
item.description: IIR met Abraham Izquierdo, Managing Director of Treasury Risk, Banorte, at SAS Innovate 2026 in Grapevine, Texas, where SAS introduced him as a customer whose work in banking risk management had relevance beyond banking. Izquierdo described how Banorte is using SAS technology, core banking data and in-house machine learning to connect liquidity-risk monitoring with external sentiment signals. “One of those is risk management.” From Balance Sheet Modeling to Broader Risk Analytics Banorte’s AI-enabled liquidity-risk initiative builds on a broader risk analytics program that has included balance sheet modeling and interest-rate risk management, areas where the institution has used SAS technology to model banking-book positions and analyze the behavior of assets, liabilities and hedges.

item.title: Guardian Builds AI-Ready Talent for Enterprise Change
item.description: (Guardian Life reception area with views of the High Line at Hudson Yards. Image source: Perkins & Will.) Insurance technology professionals had little reason to expect the pace of change to slow. AI has introduced a new degree of acceleration, compressing what had been a steady evolution of skills into a more urgent reconsideration of technology talent, operating models and business accountability.

item.title: Calling Cops Delay Doesn’t Wreck UM Claim, West Virginia High Court Rules
item.description: But that’s not enough to negate a claim, the West Virginia Supreme Court of Appeals decided this week. “… We conclude that, under the clear and unambiguous language of the subject policy, West Virginia National was required to prove that its investigative interests were prejudiced by petitioners’ failure to report the accident to a police, peace, or judicial officer within twenty-four hours,” the high court wrote in its May 21 opinion . The couple did report the crash to the insurance company within four days, and West Virginia National began its own investigation of the claim.

item.title: Airbus, Air France Found Guilty in 2009 Rio-Paris Crash
item.description: Airbus SE and Air France were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the deadly crash of a passenger jet en route to Paris from Rio de Janeiro 17 years ago after a French appeals court overturned a previous ruling. Top photo: Investigators inspect debris from the mid-Atlantic crash of Air France flight 447 at the CEAT aeronautical laboratory in Toulouse, France in 2009. The companies were solely culpable for the accident that killed all 228 passengers and crew on board the Airbus A330, the Paris appeals court said on Thursday.

item.title: Years Before UPS Plane Crash, 10 Similar Flaws Didn’t Raise Alarm
item.description: There has been only one other crash involving a similar plane model when the engine fell off decades earlier. After the engine flew off a UPS cargo plane and caused a crash that killed 15 people in Kentucky, investigators quickly discovered cracks in parts of the engine mount. Then they found records showing similar flaws had been found during maintenance on the wings of 10 other planes — most of which had not been reported to the FAA.

item.title: Waymo Suspends Freeway Rides Across U.S., Pauses Atlanta Operations
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo said on Thursday it has suspended its robotaxi service on freeways in the United States and paused its operations in Atlanta, Georgia, as it updates software to improve performance around construction zones and flooded roadways. “We have temporarily paused freeway operations, as we work to integrate recent technical learnings into our software and expect to resume these routes soon,” a Waymo spokesperson said in an email. Freeway trips on Waymo were available in San Francisco, Los Angeles, Phoenix, and Miami.

item.title: 45M Americans Planning Memorial Day Weekend Getaways: AAA
item.description: “Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer, and for most Americans, it’s a three-day weekend,” said Stacey Barber, vice president of AAA Travel. This Memorial Day, Americans will set a new record, with 45 million expected to travel over the holiday period, according to the auto membership organization AAA. “Travel demand remains strong, and despite higher fuel prices, many people are prioritizing leisure travel during holiday breaks.” Memorial Day Travelers by Mode of Transportation By Car: AAA projects 39.1 million people will travel by car over Memorial Day weekend, a slight increase over last year.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Massive Deals For Medical Devices, Futuristic AI Gadgets And Frontier Labs Lead
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . Want to keep track of the largest startup funding deals in 2026 with our curated list of $100 million-plus venture deals to U.S.-based companies?

item.title: Tradecraft ILS Advisors consultancy launched by former AXIS ILS exec Kyle Freeman
item.description: Tradecraft ILS Advisors has been launched as a new independent consulting and advisory firm focused on the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market by well-known industry executive and former AXIS ILS leader Kyle Freeman. With Tradecraft ILS Advisors, Freeman aims to work with organisations to help them in their strategic decision-making, transaction negotiations, contract structuring, and analysis, of insurance-linked securities opportunities and risk transfer arrangements. Under Tradecraft ILS Advisors, Freeman sees an opportunity to bring his experience in a broad range of ILS structures for multiple classes of business and perils, as well as his understanding of investor needs, to clients looking to enter, expand, or innovate in the market.

item.title: Catastrophe bond issuance in H1 2026 now projected at $16.3bn, could rise further
item.description: Catastrophe bond issuance for the first-half of 2026 is now projected to reach $16.3 billion according to the latest Artemis Deal Directory data, which could rise further as there remain a number of offerings in the market which could upsize, while further new deals could emerge. Catastrophe bond issuance in 2026 has continued to accelerate and the addition of State Farm’s new $1.5 billion of Merna Re Enterprise cat bonds yesterday has given issuance a meaningful boost. Within the 144A cat bond bucket, over $13.72 billion of issuance settled so far in 2026 are property catastrophe bonds, while only $384.5 million cover specialty or life/health related business .

item.title: Markel International to modernise underwriting environment with hyperexponential partnership
item.description: Markel International, a division of Markel Insurance, the insurance operations within Markel Group Inc., has partnered with hyperexponential (hx) to modernise rating, underwriting workflows and integration architecture across its Canadian business. By integrating hx’s platform, Markel is investing heavily in a sophisticated, AI-native underwriting environment. As part of its collaboration with hyperexponential, Markel Canada has launched a purpose-built Environmental rating capability on the hx platform, enabling a more streamlined, digital experience.

item.title: Aon appoints Alberto Carrion as Head of Lenders Solutions
item.description: Aon, a global professional services firm providing risk, retirement and health solutions, has appointed Alberto Carrion as Head of Lenders Solutions. In this role at Aon, Carrion will support the development of the firm’s Lenders Solutions offering, working with lending clients to address risk considerations and capital priorities as part of Aon’s wider advisory capabilities. Across his role at Aon, his focus will be on strengthening how the firm supports lenders in managing complex risk environments and delivering tailored solutions to clients.

item.title: Delay in Calling Police Does Not Wreck UM Claim, WV Supreme Court Says
item.description: But that’s not enough to negate a claim, the West Virginia Supreme Court of Appeals decided this week. “… We conclude that, under the clear and unambiguous language of the subject policy, West Virginia National was required to prove that its investigative interests were prejudiced by petitioners’ failure to report the accident to a police, peace, or judicial officer within twenty-four hours,” the high court wrote in its May 21 opinion . The auto insurer soon denied the uninsured motorist claim, based exclusively on the policyholder’s failure to report to police within 24 hours, per the policy wording.

item.title: Gov. Newsom Issues Executive Order to Prep Workers, Businesses for AI Disruption
item.description: Gavin Newsom issued an executive order to attempt to deal with the impacts of artificial intelligence on workers and businesses. Related: Acrisure to Cut 2,250 Employees, Citing Advances in Technology and AI The order directs the state to explore policies that include severance standards, employment insurance and transition support for displaced workers, worker ownership models, universal basic capital concepts, expanded workforce training and better tracking of hiring and payroll trends to help respond to potential layoffs and economic disruption. The order directs the certain state agencies to: The order mandates that within within 90 days the Labor and Workforce Development Agency and other state agencies in consultation with academic and private industry partners must provide a review of emerging research identifying the potential workforce impacts of technological shifts, including AI’s impact on California’s labor market and potential disproportionate impacts on demographic groups.

item.title: People Moves: Markel Taps Talbot’s O’Donoghue to Lead Fine Art & Specie for London; Aon’s Global ReSpecialty Team Promotes Mitchell, Hires Floodflash’s Rimmer
item.description: Markel Taps Talbot’s O’Donoghue to Lead Fine Art & Specie for London Operations Markel Insurance , the insurance operation within Markel Group Inc., announced the appointment of Danny O’Donoghue as head of Fine Art & Specie, effective immediately. O’Donoghue will be responsible for leading the strategic and sustainable expansion of Markel’s fine art & specie portfolio to further enhance the organization’s position in London as a fine art & specie insurer. Previously, he was employed at Talbot as head of Fine Art & Specie, where he led global strategy and execution for Fine Art & Specie across Lloyd’s Syndicate 1183 and AIG company platforms.

item.title: Flow apuesta por tap to phone para expandir pagos físicos en LatAm, sin desplegar terminales
item.description: La fintech chilena Flow quiere convertir el smartphone en la próxima terminal de cobro para comercios en América Latina y, para lograrlo, trabaja en el despliegue de la tecnología tap to phone en Chile, México y Perú. Con esta apuesta, la empresa busca entrar con más fuerza al mundo presencial sin depender del modelo tradicional de terminales físicas. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Viewpoint: Why Speedy Payouts Matter in EU Plan to Close Insurance Protection Gap
item.description: With a lot of recent climate events, insurance protection gaps have appeared, further complicating the financial implications of weather volatility. A recent report from the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority (EIOPA) pointed to growing protection gaps for floods, earthquakes and wildfires, highlighting Greece and Italy as the nations with the highest total protection gaps for natural disasters. Catastrophe insurance data provider PERILS recently increased its initial loss estimate as a result of the storm from €467 million to €479 million ($548.5 million to $562.6 million).

item.title: Cyber Insurance Market Faces Pressure as Claims Severity Climbs
item.description: While the cyber insurance market is seeing more capacity and increased competition, insurers are still battling with the challenge of increasing frequency and severity of claims, according to John Menefee, enterprise cyber lead at Travelers Bond and Specialty Insurance, in a conversation with Carrier Management at Travelers’ New York City office. Everyone is fighting for market share on new business at a time where there’s been an increase in frequency and severity of claims.” “It’s been a soft market for a few years running, and 2025 was a continuation of that,” he said.

item.title: Aon, Zurich, Howden, Sompo: 11 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Mid-year renewals seen down 15-20%+, cat bonds more of a competitive threat: Dutt, Aeolus
item.description: The mid-year 2026 property catastrophe reinsurance renewals are seeing pricing outcomes skewing towards the worst end of a -15% to-20%+ range, while catastrophe bonds have become more of a competitive threat to reinsurers now given longer-durations of cover and better rates, according to Aditya Dutt, President of Aeolus Capital Management. At the mid-year renewals, property catastrophe reinsurance pricing is seen to be down 15% to 20% compared to the prior year, a faster decline than the 10% to 15% declines seen at January 1st. But Dutt told the analysts that outcomes are skewing more towards the worst end of that range, with pricing pressure most intense in US nationwide accounts and the Florida market cited as the most competitive.

item.title: Allstate starts 2026 annual aggregate risk period with $870m of April catastrophe losses
item.description: US primary insurer Allstate has begun the new annual aggregate risk period, that its catastrophe bonds and excess of loss reinsurance are subject to, with a reported $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses in April 2026. The risk period for Allstate’s annual aggregate catastrophe bonds under its Sanders Re program, which sit in the nationwide aggregate tower, begins on April 1st each year. It’s worth highlighting that Allstate said that the $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses from April 2026, which translates to $687 million after-tax, were as a result of 10 wind and hail events.

item.title: Three new P&C insurers enter recovering Florida market: OIR
item.description: Three new property and casualty (P&C) insurers have entered Florida’s market as it continues to recover following historic legislative reforms, the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation (OIR) has announced. The additions of Builder Reciprocal Insurance Exchange, Frontline Insurance Reciprocal Exchange and Wingsail Insurance Company brings the total number of insurers entering the Florida market to 20 since the state implemented the reforms. However, following aggressive legislative overhauls aimed at curbing predatory lawsuits and stabilising the market in Florida, insurers are now beginning to return to the state.

item.title: Marsh re-elects full slate of Board Members for one year period
item.description: Marsh, a global firm specialising in insurance broking and risk management, has re-elected its entire slate of 13 Board members as per its discussion during the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Stockholders have elected the entire slate of 2026 director nominees for a one-year term expiring at next year’s annual meeting. Last month, Marsh’s global reinsurance broker Guy Carpenter generated revenue of $1.2 billion in the first quarter of 2026, up 3%, or 2% year-on-year on an underlying basis, supporting a robust start to the year for the broking group.

item.title: One Alliance North America enters cat bond market with $115m issuance through One Shield Re
item.description: One Alliance North America Insurance Company, formerly known as Universal North America Insurance Company, has entered the catastrophe bond market through One Shield Re Ltd., its newly established Bermuda-based Special Purpose Insurer. By establishing a long-term platform to access collateralized reinsurance backed by capital markets, One Shield Re aims to diversify One Alliance North America’s reinsurance capacity sources. “It was a privilege to partner with One Alliance North America on their inaugural catastrophe bond,” says Zach Breslin, Head of Lockton Re Capital Markets.

item.title: OSHA Has 6 Inspectors for 60,000 West Virginia Workplaces
item.description: Today, just six federal OSHA inspectors oversee workplace safety for more than 695,000 workers employed across roughly 60,000 workplaces in West Virginia, according to the AFL-CIO’s annual “Death on the Job” report. In fact, the facility had not been inspected again before the fatal incident, highlighting a problem with workplace safety in West Virginia and across the country: federal inspectors do not regularly inspect the most dangerous workplaces. OSHA, or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration, conducts thousands of workplace inspections every year, including hundreds at workplaces in West Virginia.

item.title: These Homeowners Got an Energy-Bill Lifeline. Trump Yanked It Back
item.description: “And they’re not.” “Since day one, President Trump has made American energy dominance a top priority,” EPA Press Secretary Brigit Hirsch said in a statement. The energy demands are a burden on low-income homeowners, who struggle to afford repairs to roofs and windows that would make their homes more airtight and moderate their bills. But after President Donald Trump returned to office in 2025, his Environmental Protection Agency moved to cancel roughly $1.6 billion in previously awarded grants, including the one Holloway was counting on.

item.title: Acrisure to Cut 2,250 Employees, Citing Advances in Technology and AI
item.description: Acrisure did not immediately respond to a request for comment but Williams’ memo said the company is entering a “new phase of execution.” “Advances in technology, [artificial intelligence], and digital platforms are fundamentally changing how businesses operate, how clients expect to be served, and how value is created,” he said. “Leveraging technology must be the core of how we operate, how we grow, and how we delver value to clients every day,” he explained to employees, adding that North America insurance operations will organize “more intentionally around our lines of business.” In October 2025, Acrisure cited advancements in technology and AI when it said it was laying off 400 members of its accounting workforce this year. Acrisure’s CEO told employees that about 2,250 of them won’t be with the company by the end of 2027.

item.title: HR Path acquires Mexico-based TEC360 People
item.description: HR Path , a France-based provider of HR consulting and HRIS solutions, announced the acquisition of TEC360 People, a Mexican company specializing in HR technology and talent solutions. “The acquisition of TEC360 People, a company recognized for its expertise in Cornerstone and SAP Incentive solutions, supports our ambition to become the global reference in HR transformation.” – Frederic Van Bellinghen, Partner at HR Path. “As we continue to focus on the development of our core business within TEC360 Group, joining HR Path ensures continuity for our clients, expanded opportunities for our people, and access to a truly global platform.” – Peter Wiegandt, CEO & Founder of TEC360 Group.

item.title: Allianz expands partnership with supermarket chain Asda
item.description: Allianz UK and Asda are expanding their partnership to launch home and motor insurance products for Asda customers in summer 2026. The partnership builds on a relationship that began more than 15 years ago when Allianz provided Britannia Rescue breakdown coverage to Asda customers. Under the agreement, Asda will market the products while Allianz UK will handle administration, underwriting, and claims management.

item.title: Patriot unveils new brand identity
item.description: Patriot Growth Insurance Services announced a new brand identity as the brokerage continues expanding its national platform and capabilities across property & casualty, employee benefits, personal insurance, risk management, and consulting. Founded in 2019, Patriot said the refreshed branding is designed to reflect its evolution into a more unified national organization while maintaining the local relationships and expertise of its partner agencies. “Patriot has always been built on relationships.

item.title: BBVA Seguros, Ibercaja y Axa aceleran el crecimiento en primas de Vida
item.description: En un contexto en el que el seguro de Vida registró un crecimiento medio del 9,5% en su volumen de primas facturaras en el primer trimestre , varios grupos han superado ampliamente ese umbral, destacando incrementos por encima del doble dígito, según el ranking de Icea. BBVA Seguros avanzó un 23,64% en sus ingresos, consolidando su posición entre los cuatro primeros puestos. Ibercaja fue otro de los protagonistas, con un crecimiento del 23,92%, aunque quizá el dato más llamativo corresponde a Axa , que disparó su facturación un 43,41% en el periodo.

item.title: Helix Marine celebra su 5º aniversario y anuncia su entrada en Portugal
item.description: Helix Marine celebró ayer en Madrid el acto conmemorativo de su 5º aniversario, en el que participaron más de un centenar de profesionales del sector. En su 5º aniversario, Helix Marine quiso también homenajear a varios profesionales del sector, entre los que se encontraba la periodista de INESE, Elena Benito , que fue además quien pronunció las palabras de agradecimiento en nombre de todos ellos. Fue el marco en el que la agencia de suscripción anunció su expansión por la Unión Europea y América Latina (teniendo a Portugal como primer paso, donde ya cuenta incluso con delegado) , y la entrada en nuevas líneas de actividad (como adelantamos hace unos días, ha obtenido autorización para operar en el segmento de Puertos y Terminales), entre ellas Energía , en principio en RC y donde luego ampliará a Ciber, D&O, Daños y Travel.

item.title: Las empresas españolas acusan un déficit en la definición de su propuesta de valor al empleado
item.description: Según el informe, solo el 19% de las empresas en España cuenta con una propuesta de valor al empleado (EVP) definida. Las organizaciones españolas presentan una madurez en el uso de datos superior a la media mundial, pero esa ventaja no se traduce en una gestión más proactiva del capital humano . Así lo concluye el primer ‘Human Capital Trends Study’, publicado por Aon , que analiza el estado de las estrategias de personas en un contexto de aceleración de la inteligencia artificial.
